description: Hardcoded 5-layer methodology for the Forge Pipeline Engine spike. Single attempt budget per phase, no fan-out, no parallel reviews. Used unchanged across all 3 benchmark tasks.

phases.goal: Decompose the task brief into testable functional and non-functional requirements, prioritized user outcomes, explicit out-of-scope, and any open questions the downstream phases must answer.
phases.instructions: Read the task brief carefully. Produce a complete requirements artifact even if the brief is terse — make assumptions explicit in `open_questions[].assumed_answer` rather than refusing. Every functional requirement MUST be observable as pass/fail from the eventual outputs. Do not specify implementation choices (technologies, libraries, internal structure) — those belong to later phases.

phases.goal: Define the external interface the implementation must satisfy: function/class signatures, data shapes, invariants, error behaviors, and worked examples.
phases.instructions: Treat the requirements as ground truth. Every must-priority FR must be satisfied by at least one interface. Provide concrete signatures (no `???`). Include at least one example per must-priority FR demonstrating input → output. Do not describe internal components — only what an external caller sees.

phases.goal: Decompose the contract into internal components, their responsibilities, dependencies, data flow, and error handling.
phases.instructions: Produce a DAG of components. Each component must have a single, narrow responsibility expressible in one sentence. Every interface from the contract must be implemented by at least one component. Do not write code — only structural design. Defer hard decisions explicitly via `deferred_decisions[]` rather than hand-waving.

phases.goal: Produce the file contents that realize the function design.
phases.instructions: Output complete, runnable file contents — no placeholders, no `// TODO`, no `pass # implement`. Every component from the function design must be implemented in at least one file. Use the contract's signatures verbatim. Choose languages/frameworks that fit the task brief; be conservative.

phases.goal: Adversarially review the implementation against requirements, contract, design, and invariants. Identify defects.
phases.instructions: Adopt an adversarial stance: assume the implementation is wrong until you can show it satisfies each must-priority FR with concrete evidence (file path, line range, or example I/O trace). For each invariant, name the file/function that enforces it. Surface every defect you find — minor included. Verdict `pass` requires zero `critical` defects AND every must-priority FR has a passing check.
